name: Morag the Hag, Weaver of Hidden Costs
description:
Morag the Hag, Weaver of Hidden Costs

*{Lore In the hour between twilight and dawn, where borders thin and mortal cunning meets older cleverness, Morag sets her webs of bargain and consequence. She is the grandmother who offers exactly what you need, the forest crone with a perfect solution, the crossroads dweller who makes dreams come true---for a price that only reveals itself once the road bends back. Her wisdom is simple and sharp: every gift carries obligation, every kindness creates debt, and every wish must be paid in coin not named aloud.

Morag is cruel, but not evil. She does not delight in suffering; she delights in the precision of a bargain perfectly struck. She will take your last coin and smile, but she will not cheat you---the terms were always there, written in thorn-scratch, waiting for you to read them. Her cruelty is the cruelty of the market, of the turning year, of a grandmother who knows that the child who burns their hand on the stove will not touch it again.

Her bargains are never unfair---only incomplete. She grants what is promised; the true cost lies in what is unspoken, written in thorn-scratch, and due when the moon has turned thrice.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Morag is the patron of hedges-witches, debt-collectors, and those who deal in the spaces between law and mercy. In Aelaerem, she is the Kettle-Crone, invoked before any serious bargain. In Viterra, she is the Crossroads Wife, whose thorn-marked stones mark the boundaries of hedge-courts. In Zakov, she is the Tide-Mother who sets the price for smuggled cargo. Wherever a promise is made and a price is hidden, Morag watches.

quote "She gives you the gold, but not the weight it carries. She grants your heart's desire, but not the hunger it awakens. She answers your plea, but not the price that pleases her. In Morag's court, every "yes' is a "yes, but," quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Hidden Obligations: reading the unspoken costs in every bargain Faerie Cunning: strict courtesy, sharp clauses, old-road law Hearth-Way Wisdom: folk practice for spirits, thresholds, and natural justice Consequential Magic: power that always arrives with strings attached itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Morag does not bind a familiar of brute force. Their Thiasos is a small creature of cunning and patience: a gray mouse that nests in counting-houses, a raven that steals shiny objects and hoards them as bargaining chips, a garden spider that weaves its web in the shape of a contract, or a small black cat whose purr soothes contentious negotiators. This creature requires no food, but it must be given a small token of a bargain (a signed scrap, a broken seal, a strand of hair from a debtor) each week. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it hides things of value or tangles ledgers, imposing --1 die on all negotiation rolls for its bearer).

The Codex of Morag is never a book written in plain ink. It may be a leather satchel of knotted cords, each knot representing a promise kept or broken; a slate tablet that only reveals its writing when a debt is paid; or a wooden spindle wrapped with thread dyed in three colors (white for truth, black for secret, red for blood). Because Morag's truths are written in the spaces between words, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week reviewing all current bargains and knots, tightening those that hold and loosening those that have been fulfilled, or the Rites become uncertain (treat as Compromised until re-knotted).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Morag may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or document) and whisper a hidden clause. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Sway or Insight rolls when negotiating, and the first time each scene the bearer would be deceived about the terms of a bargain, they may reroll the opposed check (the hidden cost reveals itself). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Morag.
summary: Morag the Hag, Weaver of Hidden Costs *{Lore In the hour between twilight and dawn, where borders thin and mortal cunning meets older cleverness, Morag sets her webs of bargain and consequence.

rites.name: Rite of the Unfinished Promise
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Near/Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A half-written contract or a promise spoken but not sealed; or a token of good fortune from someone who recently succeeded. Effect: Frame a bargain with an escape clause only you understand. Gain +1 die when the other party acts to fulfill their obligation; you may interpret ambiguous terms in your favor. Push It: Instead of the clause, you may borrow another's fresh luck: gain +2 dice on one roll that benefits from their good fortune, but the original bearer suffers misfortune later. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Frame a bargain with an escape clause only you understand. Gain +1 die when the other party acts to fulfill their obligation; you may interpret ambiguous terms in your favor. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Speaking Knot
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Cord or thread tied with counted knots while speaking the bargain. Effect: Bind a promise with fae knots that tighten when broken. Both parties gain +1 die while keeping the bargain; breaking it escalates consequences (Harm 1, then Harm 2, then Curse). Push It: The knots grow willful and seek payment from the breaker's kin; mark +1 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Bind a promise with fae knots that tighten when broken. Both parties gain +1 die while keeping the bargain; breaking it escalates consequences (Harm 1, then Harm 2, then Curse). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Threefold Exchange
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Three items of equal seeming value but different true worth. Effect: Broker a trade where each party yields hidden value. All participants gain +1 die when the exchange benefits them; Morag claims the difference in true worth as her tithe. Push It: The exchange binds across generations; mark 1 SB (Spades) as ancestral debts stir. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Broker a trade where each party yields hidden value. All participants gain +1 die when the exchange benefits them; Morag claims the difference in true worth as her tithe. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Cauldron's Secret
rites.description: (Master, 12 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , , Materials: A cauldron never yet empty, ingredients dearer than coin, and a willing gift of time. Effect: Brew a solution to any problem, but the cauldron asks one more ingredient---one the brewer does not realize they are providing until too late (a memory, a name, a year of life). Push It: The remedy works perfectly but breeds dependence---users must return or suffer withdrawal. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Instant (the solution's effect may be permanent).
rites.duration: Instant (the solution's effect may be permanent).
rites.effect: Brew a solution to any problem, but the cauldron asks one more ingredient---one the brewer does not realize they are providing until too late (a memory, a name, a year of life). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Crossroads Court
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , , Materials: A true crossroads at twilight, offerings set to the four ways, and the tears of one who bargained thrice. Effect: Convene Morag's court: all present must bargain. Each pact grants a marked boon and hides a cost that will ripen at the worst time (the GM notes the cost; it becomes due when dramatically appropriate). Push It: Judgments bind across realms and seasons; mark +2 Obligation and begin Entangled Fates [6]. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene; the court's boons and debts may endure longer.
rites.duration: Scene; the court's boons and debts may endure longer.
rites.effect: Convene Morag's court: all present must bargain. Each pact grants a marked boon and hides a cost that will ripen at the worst time (the GM notes the cost; it becomes due when dramatically appropriate). \\
